title: Didymoplexis cinnabarina
intro: Didymoplexis cinnabarina, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a rare and endangered orchid species found in the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Didymoplexis cinnabarina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Didymoplexis cinnabarina is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its commercial availability and usage are limited due to its rarity and endangered status.
taxonomy: Didymoplexis cinnabarina is a species of orchid, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Orchidaceae, subfamily Orchidoideae, tribe Cranichideae, subtribe Goodyerinae. This placement is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic studies. Within the genus Didymoplexis, D. cinnabarina is one of the several species found in Southeast Asia.
